title: ficus cumingii var. angustissima
intro: Ficus cumingii var. angustissima, also known as Balete, is a variety of the Ficus cumingii tree, native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia, and is characterized by its narrow leaves and small stature. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and wounds, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ficus cumingii var. angustissima include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable resource for treating various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are applied topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
